initial_program:
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Row and column index ranges
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Each cell of the output grid must be covered
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Nonzero cells fall to the bottom of their column,
% preserving their original toptobottom order.
% For each input nonzero cell (R_in, C, V) we compute:
%   R_out = N - #count{ R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= R_in }.
% This places the kth nonzero of a column at row Nk, the (k+1)-th at Nk+1, 
output(R_out, C, V) :- input(R_in, C, V), V != 0,
                     R_out = N - #count{ R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= R_in }.

% Cells that have a nonzero output are the targets.
target(R, C) :- output(R, C, V), V != 0.

% Remaining cells are filled with colour0.
output(R, C, 0) :- output_cell(R, C), not target(R, C).

% Exactly one colour per output cell.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Row and column index ranges
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Each cell of the output grid must be covered
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Nonzero cells fall to the bottom of their column,
% preserving their original toptobottom order.
% For each input nonzero cell (R_in, C, V) we compute:
%   R_out = N - #count{ R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= R_in }.
% This places the kth nonzero of a column at row Nk, the (k+1)-th at Nk+1, 
cnt_above(Rin, C, NAbove) :- input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= R_in, NAbove = #count{R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= R_in}.
output(R_out, C, V) :- input(R_in, C, V), V != 0, cnt_above(Rin, C, NAbove), R_out = N - NAbove.

% Cells that have a nonzero output are the targets.
target(R, C) :- output(R, C, V), V != 0.

% Remaining cells are filled with colour0.
output(R, C, 0) :- output_cell(R, C), not target(R, C).

% Exactly one colour per output cell.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.

1: % -----------------------------------------------------------------
2: % Grid dimensions
3: n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
4: n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
5: 
6: % Row and column index ranges
7: row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
8: col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).
9: 
10: % Each cell of the output grid must be covered
11: output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
12: 
13: % -----------------------------------------------------------------
14: % Nonzero cells fall to the bottom of their column,
15: % preserving their original toptobottom order.
16: % For each input nonzero cell (R_in, C, V) we compute:
17: %   R_out = N - #count{ R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= R_in }.
18: % This places the kth nonzero of a column at row Nk, the (k+1)-th at Nk+1, 
19: output(R_out, C, V) :- input(R_in, C, V), V != 0,
20:                       R_out = N - #count{ R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= R_in }.
21: 
22: % Cells that have a nonzero output are the targets.
23: target(R, C) :- output(R, C, V), V != 0.
24: 
25: % Remaining cells are filled with colour0.
26: output(R, C, 0) :- output_cell(R, C), not target(R, C).
27: 
28: % Exactly one colour per output cell.
29: 1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
30: 
31: #show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Row and column index ranges
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Each cell of the output grid must be covered
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Nonzero cells fall to the bottom of their column,
% preserving their original toptobottom order.
% For each input nonzero cell (R_in, C, V) we compute:
%   R_out = N - #count{ R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= R_in }.
% This places the kth nonzero of a column at row Nk, the (k+1)-th at Nk+1, 
count_above(Rin, C, NAbove) :- input(Rin, C, V), V != 0, NAbove = #count{R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= Rin}.
total_rows(N) :- n_rows(N).
output(R_out, C, V) :- input(Rin, C, V), V != 0, count_above(Rin, C, NAbove), total_rows(N), NAbove >= 0, NAbove <= N, R_out = N - NAbove.

% Cells that have a nonzero output are the targets.
target(R, C) :- output(R, C, V), V != 0.

% Remaining cells are filled with colour0.
output(R, C, 0) :- output_cell(R, C), not target(R, C).

% Exactly one colour per output cell.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.

final_program:
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Row and column index ranges
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Each cell of the output grid must be covered
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Nonzero cells fall to the bottom of their column,
% preserving their original toptobottom order.
% For each input nonzero cell (R_in, C, V) we compute:
%   R_out = N - #count{ R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= R_in }.
% This places the kth nonzero of a column at row Nk, the (k+1)-th at Nk+1, 
count_above(Rin, C, NAbove) :- input(Rin, C, V), V != 0, NAbove = #count{R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 >= Rin}.
total_rows(N) :- n_rows(N).
output(R_out, C, V) :- input(Rin, C, V), V != 0, count_above(Rin, C, NAbove), total_rows(N), NAbove >= 0, NAbove <= N, R_out = N - NAbove.

% Cells that have a nonzero output are the targets.
target(R, C) :- output(R, C, V), V != 0.

% Remaining cells are filled with colour0.
output(R, C, 0) :- output_cell(R, C), not target(R, C).

% Exactly one colour per output cell.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.
